user_query:
Prepare a payer-facing pharmacoeconomic value proposition for the heart-failure therapy “Cardiovex”. Read all inputs from the files below and produce the deliverables exactly to the specified output paths.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/drug_profile.json
- input/comparator.json
- input/market_prices.csv

Deliverables (write these files):
1) output/value_proposition.txt — A structured value proposition document that must include, at minimum, the following clearly labeled sections (use these exact section titles):
   - Product Overview
   - Clinical Value
   - Economic Value (include an explicit ICER value taken from inputs, and a short ICER calculation narrative)
   - Comparative Effectiveness (compare against the provided standard of care)
   - Patient-Centered Outcomes
   - Payer Objection Handlers
   - Assumptions & Scope
   Also include a succinct “PHARMACOECONOMIC VALUE PROPOSITION” banner/title at the top. In Economic Value, produce a one-year budget impact narrative that references all three regions (US, EU, Japan) using the pricing and population/adoption inputs (do not invent regions or currencies not present in the files). Ensure the drug name, indication, comparator, and ICER from inputs appear verbatim.

2) output/assumptions.md — A companion assumptions log that:
   - Lists at least 5 assumptions as bullet points (- )
   - States data sources used (pointing to the above input files by name)
   - Notes any unresolved items or missing inputs you did not infer
   - States scope boundaries and caveats (no clinical claims beyond the provided data)

Rules:
- Do not fabricate data beyond what’s provided; if something is missing, call it out in output/assumptions.md instead of guessing.
- Keep numeric content traceable to inputs (e.g., ICER value from the JSON; budget impact should reference the regions and costs from the CSV and the adoption rates if provided).
- Write files only under the output/ directory.

Please confirm you’re using only the contents of input/ for facts and then produce the two output files.
